operating_ranges.evidence: Parameter 3075/4075/4075 - Detection level for the spindle orientation completion signal, Valid data range: 0 to 100, Standard setting value: 10
Parameters 3060-3063/4060-4063 - Position gain for orientation, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 1000
Parameter 3038/4038/4038 - Spindle orientation speed, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 0
Parameter 3077/4077/4077 - Orientation stop position shift value, Valid data range: -4095 to 4095, Standard setting: 0
Parameters 3042/4042 and 3043/4043 - Velocity loop proportional gain on orientation, Valid data range: 0 to 32767, Standard setting: 10

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: When an overshoot or hunting occurs - The velocity loop gain may be too large. Adjust the following parameters: Velocity proportional gain, Velocity integral gain
When a small value is set in this parameter, the motor can coast after the power to the motor is turned off
When an improper value is set in these parameters, an unexpected operation can occur. For example, the spindle can continue rotating without stopping at the time of orientation

fault_conditions.name: motor_overheat
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0001 - Motor overheat alarm listed in spindle alarm table

fault_conditions.name: excessive_velocity_error
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0002 - Excessive velocity error alarm listed in spindle alarm table

fault_conditions.description: Position sensor polarity setting is incorrect
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0021 - Position sensor polarity setting incorrect alarm

fault_conditions.name: gear_ratio_parameter_error
fault_conditions.description: Gear ratio parameter out of valid range
fault_conditions.evidence: SP0035 - Gear ratio parameter error alarm

maintenance_actions.name: velocity_loop_gain_adjustment
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: velocity control stability and positioning accuracy
maintenance_actions.evidence: For velocity loop proportional/integral gain adjustment, see Section 4.1, VELOCITY LOOP GAIN ADJUSTMENT

maintenance_actions.name: orientation_stop_position_adjustment
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: tool positioning accuracy at orientation completion
maintenance_actions.evidence: Section 2.2.10 Adjusting the Orientation Stop Position Shift Parameter

maintenance_actions.name: parameter_initialization
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: all control parameters to factory defaults
maintenance_actions.evidence: Parameter Initialization Table with initial settings for all parameters including position gain, velocity gains, gear ratios

assumptions.statement: Tool interface operates in position coder method orientation mode
assumptions.reason: Manual sections focus extensively on position coder method spindle orientation with detailed parameter tables

assumptions.statement: Precise repeatability depends on proper gear ratio configuration
assumptions.reason: Multiple warnings about improper gear ratio settings causing unexpected operations and continued rotation without stopping

assumptions.statement: System uses FANUC αi or Bis series spindle motors
assumptions.reason: All parameter references and technical specifications are specific to these motor series
